title: Duplicate check and insert are not atomic
reasoning: The command checks for an existing monthly contribution and then inserts in a separate query. Two overlapping invocations for the same family and period can both observe no existing row before either insert commits, causing duplicate obligations unless a database constraint outside the included files prevents it. Even with a constraint, this path would surface as an unhandled duplicate-key failure instead of a clean skip.
reproduction: Start two contributions:generate processes for the same family/month when no contribution exists for a member; both can pass exists() before either creates the row.
recommendation: Enforce uniqueness for family_id, user_id, year, and month at the database level, and use an atomic create-or-skip path such as firstOrCreate/upsert with duplicate-key handling.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided, and ordinary single-process command tests would not exercise overlapping command execution.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a regression test for the uniqueness invariant on family_id, user_id, year, and month, plus a command test proving repeated runs do not create duplicates.
minimumFixScope: Add the uniqueness invariant and update GenerateMonthlyContributions to rely on an atomic write path.
